title: MCP, tokens, and project terms
intro: Quick check on the definitions above.
feedbackIncorrect: Review the definitions — try again on the next page if needed.

questions.prompt: Which best describes MCP (Model Context Protocol) in this course?
choices: A system that structures, manages, and transmits context between you, your codebase, and an AI model so assistance can be accurate and context-aware
A file format for packaging trained neural network weights for deployment
A GitHub feature that automatically resolves merge conflicts in pull requests
A browser extension that replaces the Canvas gradebook UI

questions.prompt: What is a personal access token (PAT) primarily for?
choices: Authenticating to services like GitHub from external tools or scripts without sharing your real password, often with limited scope and the ability to revoke
Encrypting all web traffic between your computer and GitHub by default
Storing your repository’s source code offline on a USB drive
Replacing two-factor authentication for all GitHub web logins

questions.prompt: In project management, what best describes a project?
choices: A temporary endeavor with defined objectives, scope, and resources, with a clear beginning and end, undertaken to produce a unique result
Any ongoing team activity that continues indefinitely without a planned finish
The complete history of every bug fix ever shipped in a product
A synonym for a single user story in a backlog

questions.prompt: What best describes a story in project management?
choices: A concise, user-centered description of desired functionality—often from the user’s perspective—that breaks work into actionable, value-focused pieces
A mandatory legal agreement between the client and the development vendor
A technical document that specifies exact database schemas and API latency targets only
A fixed annual budget line item for cloud hosting costs

questions.prompt: Why is MCP useful when working with a large codebase and an AI assistant?
choices: It helps gather and organize relevant context so the model sees high-signal information instead of random or overwhelming dumps
It guarantees the model will never hallucinate incorrect file paths
It automatically rewrites your entire repository to a new language
It replaces the need for version control entirely

questions.prompt: Which practice best matches how you should treat a GitHub PAT?
choices: Limit scopes to what you need, avoid sharing it, and revoke or rotate it if it may be exposed
Commit it to your repository so teammates can reuse it
Use one token for every service on the internet for simplicity
Store it only in public chat logs for easy retrieval

questions.prompt: How does a project typically differ from day-to-day operations?
choices: A project is temporary and aims at a defined outcome; operations keep the business running on an ongoing basis
Projects never have deadlines; operations always do
Operations never use software; projects always do
They are identical terms in agile practice

questions.prompt: What is a common purpose of writing stories during planning?
choices: To express user value and outcomes so work can be prioritized and broken down clearly
To replace all technical design documents permanently
To eliminate the need for acceptance criteria
To hide scope from stakeholders until launch

questions.prompt: In this course’s framing, what role does MCP play relative to tools like GitHub or your editor?
choices: It acts as structured glue—moving the right context between tools, your repo, and the model
It replaces GitHub with a local-only database
It is only used to grade assignments in Canvas
It stores your model weights on Canvas servers

questions.prompt: Before using a PAT with automation or MCP, what should you confirm first?
choices: The token has only the permissions required for the task and you know how to revoke it
The token must have admin access to every organization you belong to
The token should never expire
The token replaces your GitHub password for the website login only
